name: Melting Moments/Australian
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat the butter until light and creamy.
2. Add the icing sugar and beat until combined.
3. Add the cornflour, plain flour, and vanilla, and mix at slow speed until just amalgamated.
4. For firmer biscuits, add extra flour if desired.
5. Lightly grease a baking tray with butter and flour, then preheat the oven to 160°C.
6. Form balls of the mixture (about level dessertspoon) and place them on the prepared tray, leaving space for spreading.
7. Repeat with the remaining mixture.
8. Use a fork dipped in flour to make an imprint on each biscuit, flattening slightly. Alternatively, spoon the mixture directly onto the tray.
9. Place the tray in the center of the oven and bake slowly until the biscuits are lightly colored, about 10 to 15 minutes.
10. Remove from the oven using a spatula and transfer the biscuits to a cake rack.
11. To make the icing: beat the butter with a wooden spoon until smooth.
12. Add the icing sugar and beat until the sugar is almost incorporated into the butter.
13. Add the vanilla, salt, and boiling water, beating until thick and creamy. Adjust consistency with more icing sugar if needed.
14. Assemble biscuits by joining two together with a teaspoon of icing spread with a knife.
15. Allow the icing to set completely on a cooling rack before transferring to an airtight container.
